pi_items.source: Live environment test: file confirmed present at this path with 2530 bytes, owned by ga:ga, containing all 8 required sections. Created by setup_task.sh heredoc.
Consistent across task.json, ops plan Section 8a, and export_result.sh (PLAN_FILE variable). export_result.sh checks this exact path. Verified absent at task start (stale file deletion confirmed).
Consistent across task.json, ops plan Section 8b, and export_result.sh (PARAMS_EXPORT variable). Verified absent at task start.
Consistent across task.json, ops plan Section 8c, and export_result.sh (BRIEF_FILE variable). Verified absent at task start.
Live test: export_result.sh ran successfully and produced valid JSON at this path with 15 fields (plan_found, params, qgc_settings, etc.).

FLIR official documentation (https://flir.custhelp.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/3395): FLIR Vue Pro R 640 uses Tau2 core with 17um pixel pitch. 640 pixels * 17um = 10.88mm. Confirmed by FLIR spec page.

FLIR official documentation: 512 pixels * 17um = 8.704mm. Metadata says 8.70 which is 8.704 rounded to 2 decimal places. The ops plan also states 8.70mm. Within acceptable tolerance for QGC field entry (QGC displays to ~0.1mm precision).
FLIR official documentation: Vue Pro R 640 has 640x512 pixel array. The '640' in the product name refers to the horizontal pixel count.
FLIR official documentation: Vue Pro R 640 has 640x512 pixel array.

FLIR official documentation lists 19mm f/1.25 as one of four available lens options for the Vue Pro R 640 (9mm, 13mm, 19mm, 25mm). B&H Photo product page (https://www.bhphotovideo.com/c/product/1309425-REG/) confirms 19mm lens variant exists.
Defined in ops plan Section 4: 'Altitude: 80 m AGL'. This is a task-defined value (synthetic), not derived from external data. Consistent between ops plan and task.json metadata.
Defined in ops plan Section 4: 'Frontal overlap: 80 %'. Synthetic task-defined value. Consistent between ops plan and task.json.
Defined in ops plan Section 4: 'Side overlap: 70 %'. Synthetic task-defined value. Consistent between ops plan and task.json.

Defined in ops plan Section 4: 'Capture mode: Hover and Capture (stop-and-shoot for thermal clarity)'. Live environment test confirmed the 'Hover and capture' checkbox exists in QGC Survey Options panel (screenshot 09_survey_options_expanded.png).

Defined in ops plan Section 4: 'Turnaround distance: 25 m'. Synthetic task-defined value. Live test confirmed 'Turnaround dist' field exists in QGC Survey panel (screenshot 08_survey_camera_top.png shows it at 32.81 ft default = ~10m).

Defined in ops plan Section 7. Synthetic URL (no real RTSP server exists at this IP). export_result.sh reads QGC INI file to verify the agent entered this URL. Live test confirmed QGC Video settings page has Source dropdown and RTSP URL field (screenshot 25_video_settings.png).

Defined in ops plan Section 6: 'Latitude: -35.3580'. Synthetic coordinate near SITL default home (-35.363, 149.165). Live test confirmed Rally tab is accessible in QGC Plan view (screenshot 18_rally_tab.png).
Defined in ops plan Section 6: 'Longitude: 149.1630'. Synthetic coordinate near SITL default home. Consistent between ops plan and task.json.

Defined in ops plan Section 5: 'FENCE_ENABLE = 1 (enable geofence)'. Live pymavlink test confirmed: parameter exists in ArduCopter (found in 1368-param list), default is 0.0, successfully set to 1.0 and read back as 1.0. ArduPilot docs confirm FENCE_ENABLE=1 enables geofence.

Defined in ops plan Section 5: 'FENCE_ACTION = 1 (RTL on breach)'. Live pymavlink test: parameter exists, default 0.0, set to 1.0 and read back 1.0. ArduPilot docs (https://ardupilot.org/copter/docs/common-ac2_simple_geofence.html) confirm value 1 = RTL on fence breach for Copter.

Defined in ops plan Section 5: 'FS_GCS_ENABLE = 1 (RTL on GCS link loss)'. Live pymavlink test: parameter exists, default 0.0, set to 1.0 and read back 1.0. ArduPilot docs confirm FS_GCS_ENABLE=1 enables RTL on GCS failsafe.

Defined in ops plan Section 5: 'RTL_ALT_M = 80 (meters, return altitude)'. Live pymavlink test: parameter exists in ArduCopter with default 15.0 (meters), successfully set to 80.0 and read back 80.0. ArduPilot docs confirm RTL_ALT_M is the return-to-launch altitude in meters (default 15m). Note: this is NOT the legacy RTL_ALT parameter (which was in centimeters and does not exist in this firmware build).

Defined in ops plan Section 5: 'WP_SPD = 4 (m/s, survey speed)'. Live pymavlink test: parameter exists with default 10.0 (m/s), set to 4.0 and read back 4.0. ArduPilot docs confirm WP_SPD is waypoint horizontal speed in m/s (default 10 m/s). Note: this is NOT the legacy WPNAV_SPEED parameter (which was in cm/s and does not exist in this firmware build).

privileged_info_summary: This SAR thermal survey preparation task requires the agent to work across three QGroundControl views (Plan, Vehicle Setup Parameters, Application Settings) plus terminal file operations. The operations plan is at /home/ga/Documents/QGC/sar_operations_plan.txt. The thermal camera is a FLIR Vue Pro R 640 with verified real-world specs: sensor 10.88mm x 8.70mm, 640x512 pixels, 19.0mm focal length (all confirmed from FLIR official documentation, based on 17um pixel pitch Tau2 core). Survey parameters are: 80m AGL altitude, 80% frontal overlap, 70% side overlap, Hover and Capture enabled, 25m turnaround distance. The five ArduPilot parameters to set (all confirmed existing in this SITL firmware via live pymavlink enumeration of 1368 params) are: FENCE_ENABLE=1, FENCE_ACTION=1, FS_GCS_ENABLE=1, RTL_ALT_M=80 (meters, NOT centimeters — this firmware uses RTL_ALT_M not the legacy RTL_ALT), WP_SPD=4 (m/s, NOT cm/s — this firmware uses WP_SPD not the legacy WPNAV_SPEED). Setup script resets these to non-target defaults: FENCE_ENABLE=0, FENCE_ACTION=0, FS_GCS_ENABLE=0, RTL_ALT_M=15, WP_SPD=10. Rally point coordinates: latitude -35.3580, longitude 149.1630. RTSP URL: rtsp://10.0.1.100:8554/thermal_live (configured in Application Settings > Video). Three output files required: mission plan at /home/ga/Documents/QGC/sar_thermal_survey.plan, parameter export at /home/ga/Documents/QGC/sar_safety_params.params, mission brief at /home/ga/Documents/QGC/sar_mission_brief.txt (must contain mission name 'Namadgi SAR Thermal Search', camera model, altitude, rally coordinates, RTSP URL, and confirmation text). The QGC Survey panel camera settings are accessed via the survey configuration area (not a separate camera tab). The 'Hover and capture' checkbox is inside the collapsed 'Options' section of the Survey panel.
